titleStmt.title: Letter from Alfred Miller to Royal College of Physicians of Ireland, 5 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Alfred Miller

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Alfred Miller (1884-1942) to the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland (RCPI) concerning a resolution passed by the Committee of Management. The resolution recommends that the college restrict their recognition of courses given at technical schools and at the Pharmaceutical Society of Ireland.
Alfred Miller served as both the registrar of the Royal College of Surgeons (1910-1938) and as secretary to the Conjoint Board of the College of Physicians and College of Surgeons. At the outbreak of the Rising Miller instructed James Duncan, the college porter, to lock the college doors in order to deter Michael Mallin's occupying rebel force in St. Stephen's Green.

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

correspAction.placeName: Royal College of Surgeons, Stephen's Green, Dublin, Ireland

correspAction.persName: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland
correspAction.placeName: Royal College of Physicians, Kildare Street, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
CONJOINT BOARD IN IRELAND
OF THE
Royal College of Physicians and the Royal College
of Surgeons. OFFICES OF COMMITTEE OF MANAGEMENT
ROYAL COLLEGE Of PHYSICIANS SURGEONS
KILDARE STREET STEPHEN'S GREEN
DUBLIN, 5/7/1916 ALFRED MILLER,
Secretary Dear Sir, I am directed by the Committee of Management to send
you a copy of the following Resolution which was unanimously
adopted at their last Meeting :- That we recommend the Royal Colleges to restrict their
recognition of Courses of Lectures delivered at the
Technical Schools, and at the Pharmaceutical Society of
Ireland, to such Courses only as are given by day. I am,
Yours faithfully, Alfred Miller,
Secretary The Registrar,
Royal College of Physicians
Kildare St. Dublin Maer C.
7.VII.16
